question: What are the locations hazardous materials ID numbers must be displayed?

answers.text: On the trailer and shipping papers
On the package and on the shipping papers
answers.explanation: Hazardous materials ID numbers are primarily located on shipping papers and the package. Secondary locations include cargo tanks and bulk packaging.

answers.text: On bulk packaging and cargo tanks

explanation: Hazardous materials ID numbers are primarily located on shipping papers and the package. Secondary locations include cargo tanks and bulk packaging.

question: When hauling hazardous materials, you must check your dual tires:

answers.text: At the start of each trip, when you park, and each time you stop.
answers.explanation: To ensure the safe transportation of hazardous materials it is important to check the dual tires of your vehicle before the trip and anytime that you make a stop and park.

explanation: To ensure the safe transportation of hazardous materials it is important to check the dual tires of your vehicle before the trip and anytime that you make a stop and park.

question: Which signals may be used to warn a stopped vehicle which contains explosives?

answers.explanation: If parked along the road while carrying explosives, do not use warning signal indicators that use a flame, such as flares. Be sure to carry reflective triangles for such an emergency.

explanation: If parked along the road while carrying explosives, do not use warning signal indicators that use a flame, such as flares. Be sure to carry reflective triangles for such an emergency.

question: In which situation is a shipper NOT required to certify their preparation of a hazardous materials shipment?

answers.text: When a private carrier is transporting their own products
answers.explanation: A shipper is not required to certify the preparation of a shipment if they are a private carrier transporting their own products or the package is provided to the shipper by the carrier, such as a cargo tank.

answers.text: When the total weight of materials is below established thresholds
When the shipper is the federal government

explanation: A shipper is not required to certify the preparation of a shipment if they are a private carrier transporting their own products or the package is provided to the shipper by the carrier, such as a cargo tank.

question: Who is responsible for safely transporting a hazardous material shipment without delay and keeping the shipping papers in the right place?

answers.explanation: After verifying that the shipper has properly packaged and labeled the shipment, it is the driver's responsibility to properly store the paperwork, and then safely and expeditiously transport the cargo.

explanation: After verifying that the shipper has properly packaged and labeled the shipment, it is the driver's responsibility to properly store the paperwork, and then safely and expeditiously transport the cargo.

question: When transporting chlorine in a cargo tank, you must have:

answers.explanation: The transportation of chlorine requires a driver to have a full face respirator (gas mask) with cartridges approved for chlorine filtration. You must also carry an emergency kit capable of addressing any leaks in the dome cover plate fittings on the tank.

answers.text: An emergency kit for controlling leaks in fittings on the dome cover plate.

explanation: The transportation of chlorine requires a driver to have a full face respirator (gas mask) with cartridges approved for chlorine filtration. You must also carry an emergency kit capable of addressing any leaks in the dome cover plate fittings on the tank.

question: You need to warn other motorists of a stopped vehicle which has a tank used for flammable liquid or gases. You may use:

answers.explanation: If parked along the road while carrying flammable liquids, do not use warning signal indicators that use flame, such as flares. Be sure to carry reflective triangles for such an emergency.

explanation: If parked along the road while carrying flammable liquids, do not use warning signal indicators that use flame, such as flares. Be sure to carry reflective triangles for such an emergency.

question: If hazardous material is spilling from your vehicle, do not move your vehicle:

answers.explanation: If you determine that you are spilling or leaking your hazardous cargo during transportation, stop as soon as possible in a safe manner, and do not move the vehicle until the cargo is contained.

explanation: If you determine that you are spilling or leaking your hazardous cargo during transportation, stop as soon as possible in a safe manner, and do not move the vehicle until the cargo is contained.

question: A temperature-controlled trailer should never be used to transport which hazardous materials classes?

answers.explanation: Classes 1, 3, and 4 should not be transported within a temperature-controlled trailer.

explanation: Classes 1, 3, and 4 should not be transported within a temperature-controlled trailer.

question: The transport index of a radioactive material:

answers.text: Tells the degree of control needed during transportation.
answers.explanation: If class 7 radioactive materials include a Transport Index, this will determine how many packages you may carry of this type along with how close to humans and animals the radioactive materials may be during transport, this is called the degree of control. Different packages of this type may have different ratings assigned but the total of all the rates cannot exceed 50 on a single vehicle.

answers.text: Is another way of writing the weight of the package.
Is something that only the shipper needs to worry about.

explanation: If class 7 radioactive materials include a Transport Index, this will determine how many packages you may carry of this type along with how close to humans and animals the radioactive materials may be during transport, this is called the degree of control. Different packages of this type may have different ratings assigned but the total of all the rates cannot exceed 50 on a single vehicle.

question: What is the purpose of the radioactive transport index?

answers.text: To inform material handlers and transporters how long they can be near the materials per day
To inform material handlers and transporters how much control is required during transportation
answers.explanation: The radioactive transport index informs personnel how much control is required during shipping. This includes how much of the material can be transported and any restrictions related to how it is transported.

answers.text: To inform materials handlers and transporters what type of personal protection equipment must be worn when loading, unloading, and transporting

explanation: The radioactive transport index informs personnel how much control is required during shipping. This includes how much of the material can be transported and any restrictions related to how it is transported.

question: Which statement is true regarding the placarding of a vehicle carrying 500 pounds of Division 1.1 explosives and 300 pounds of Division 4.3 Dangerous When Wet?

answers.text: Display "Explosives 1.1" only as it is the most dangerous
The "Dangerous" placard may be used for the mixed materials cargo
Display placards for both "Explosives 1.1" and "Dangerous When Wet"
answers.explanation: Divisions 1.1 and 4.3 are both part of table 1, which means the appropriate placards are required regardless of the amount. This shipment would require both "Explosives 1.1" and "Dangerous When Wet".

explanation: Divisions 1.1 and 4.3 are both part of table 1, which means the appropriate placards are required regardless of the amount. This shipment would require both "Explosives 1.1" and "Dangerous When Wet".

question: Does the California Commercial Driver Handbook contain all you need to know to transport hazardous materials?

answers.text: Yes, as of the time of publication, but regulations change over time.
answers.explanation: The handbook is accurate as of the time of publication. The handbook provides all you need to know to get your endorsement based on existing regulations. Regulations change over time, and it is part of your job to keep updated on the changes.

explanation: The handbook is accurate as of the time of publication. The handbook provides all you need to know to get your endorsement based on existing regulations. Regulations change over time, and it is part of your job to keep updated on the changes.

question: Which of the following is not relevant to determining whether placards are required?

answers.text: The amount of hazardous material to be transported
The hazard class of the cargo
The expiration date of the materials
answers.explanation: Information to determine the use of placards includes the hazard class, the amount of the individual hazard class, and the total amounts of all hazardous materials to be transported.

explanation: Information to determine the use of placards includes the hazard class, the amount of the individual hazard class, and the total amounts of all hazardous materials to be transported.

question: A vehicle carrying explosives has crashed with another vehicle. You should not pull them apart until:

answers.text: The shipper's loading foreman is present/
The explosives have been placed at least 200 feet away from the vehicles and occupied buildings.
answers.explanation: If two vehicles have been intertwined as part of an accident and one of them is carrying explosives, the explosives must be removed from the vehicle before separating the vehicles. The explosives need to be at least 200 feet from the vehicles and any occupied structures. Everyone involved should remain at a safe distance.

explanation: If two vehicles have been intertwined as part of an accident and one of them is carrying explosives, the explosives must be removed from the vehicle before separating the vehicles. The explosives need to be at least 200 feet from the vehicles and any occupied structures. Everyone involved should remain at a safe distance.

question: Which combination of shipping material markings is NOT acceptable?

answers.text: Proper shipping name in roman print, instructions, and any cautions
Proper shipping name in italics, descriptive name, and specifications
answers.explanation: Acceptable markings include descriptive name, UN marks, ID number, weight, specifications, instructions, cautions, and the proper shipping name in Roman print only.

answers.text: UN marks, ID number, and descriptive name

explanation: Acceptable markings include descriptive name, UN marks, ID number, weight, specifications, instructions, cautions, and the proper shipping name in Roman print only.

question: While loading or unloading a cargo tank, a qualified person must be watching and be within ____ feet of the tank.

answers.explanation: The individual responsible for loading and unloading cargo (loading supervisor) must have a qualified person within 25 feet of the tank and monitor the loading or unloading.

explanation: The individual responsible for loading and unloading cargo (loading supervisor) must have a qualified person within 25 feet of the tank and monitor the loading or unloading.

question: A hazard class name or ID number may not be used to describe:

answers.text: A reportable quantity of a hazardous substance.

answers.explanation: HazMat classifications and IDs may only be used for identifying Hazardous Waste. WASTE may be placed on the label for hazardous materials. If shipping both hazardous and non-hazardous materials, the shipping label must identify the hazardous waste first.

explanation: HazMat classifications and IDs may only be used for identifying Hazardous Waste. WASTE may be placed on the label for hazardous materials. If shipping both hazardous and non-hazardous materials, the shipping label must identify the hazardous waste first.

question: What information is required on hazardous materials shipping papers?

answers.explanation: The shipping paperwork for all hazardous materials must include a description of the materials and emergency response information.

answers.text: The manufacturing date of the materials.
The mailing address of the receiver.

explanation: The shipping paperwork for all hazardous materials must include a description of the materials and emergency response information.

question: When a shipper is sending a mixed shipment of non-hazardous and hazardous materials, which is true of the corresponding paperwork?

answers.text: Hazardous materials should be listed first
answers.explanation: When both hazardous and non-hazardous materials are included in the same shipment, the shipper can either list the hazardous materials first, or identify them with a different color that stands out.

answers.text: Hazardous materials should be written in italics
Non-hazardous materials should be written in a contrasting color

explanation: When both hazardous and non-hazardous materials are included in the same shipment, the shipper can either list the hazardous materials first, or identify them with a different color that stands out.

question: If there is an RQ before or after the item description on the shipping paper, it means the:

answers.text: Material is in a package containing no other materials.
Carrier must report a spill of this material.
answers.explanation: The letters RQ on a shipping label refer to "Reportable Quantity". This means that the quantity indicated at the start of the trip must be the same at the end of the trip. Therefore, any leaks or spills during transportation must be reported.

answers.text: Material has an insurance value of over $1,000.

explanation: The letters RQ on a shipping label refer to "Reportable Quantity". This means that the quantity indicated at the start of the trip must be the same at the end of the trip. Therefore, any leaks or spills during transportation must be reported.

question: You have hazardous materials leaking from a container but not spilling from your vehicle. Drive to the closest area where you can get help:

answers.text: As fast as you can if it is damaging your truck.
And call emergency personnel if they are needed.
answers.explanation: Unless you have the specialized training to repackage leaking hazardous materials, do not attempt it. Move the vehicle to the closest area that will be away from people. Immediately call your dispatcher to explain the situation and get additional instructions. Call emergency personnel if the situation requires.

answers.text: In repacking the leaking materials with plastic overwrap.

explanation: Unless you have the specialized training to repackage leaking hazardous materials, do not attempt it. Move the vehicle to the closest area that will be away from people. Immediately call your dispatcher to explain the situation and get additional instructions. Call emergency personnel if the situation requires.

question: What is the purpose of placards?

answers.explanation: Placards identify the hazard class of the cargo to others so they can take appropriate action when needed.

answers.text: To prevent other drivers from passing
To allow priority access through security areas

explanation: Placards identify the hazard class of the cargo to others so they can take appropriate action when needed.

question: Which type of hazardous material will require you to refer to a transport index to determine how much of the material you are allowed to transport?

answers.explanation: Whenever you receive directions to transport Class 7 (radioactive) materials, you must refer to the transport index to determine how much you may carry

explanation: Whenever you receive directions to transport Class 7 (radioactive) materials, you must refer to the transport index to determine how much you may carry

question: May a CDL holder ever legally transport hazardous materials without an endorsement?

answers.text: Yes, when the vehicle does not require placarding
answers.explanation: A hazardous materials endorsement is required when transporting a placarded vehicle. The federal government does have exceptions for particular hazardous materials or amounts that do not require placards, and therefore do not require an endorsement.

answers.text: Yes, if the vehicle is not a CMV

explanation: A hazardous materials endorsement is required when transporting a placarded vehicle. The federal government does have exceptions for particular hazardous materials or amounts that do not require placards, and therefore do not require an endorsement.

question: It is recommended that a driver refrain from smoking or using an open flame within 25 feet of:

answers.explanation: Refrain from smoking or having an open flame within 25 feet of hazard classes 1, 2, 3, and 4. Avoid smoking in extreme proximity to any hazard class.

explanation: Refrain from smoking or having an open flame within 25 feet of hazard classes 1, 2, 3, and 4. Avoid smoking in extreme proximity to any hazard class.

question: The total transport index of all radioactive materials packages in a single vehicle must not exceed ____.

answers.explanation: If class 7 radioactive materials include a Transport Index, this will determine how many packages you may carry of this type along with how close to humans and animals the radioactive materials may be during transport. Different packages of this type may have different ratings assigned but the total of all the rates cannot exceed 50 on a single vehicle.

explanation: If class 7 radioactive materials include a Transport Index, this will determine how many packages you may carry of this type along with how close to humans and animals the radioactive materials may be during transport. Different packages of this type may have different ratings assigned but the total of all the rates cannot exceed 50 on a single vehicle.

question: Which information is unrelated to the determination of the use of hazardous materials placards?

answers.text: The total weight or volume of the material being transported
The classification or division of the hazardous material
When the material was manufactured or otherwise produced
answers.explanation: Whether to use placards or not is based on the classification of the material, how much is being shipped of each substance, and the total amount of all hazardous materials being shipped,

explanation: Whether to use placards or not is based on the classification of the material, how much is being shipped of each substance, and the total amount of all hazardous materials being shipped,

question: Which of the following would qualify as "bulk packaging"?

answers.text: A receptacle with a liquid capacity of 400 Liters (107 gallons).
A receptacle for solids with a mass of 159 kg (350 pounds)
A receptacle with a water capacity of 1,100 gallons to be used for gas.
answers.explanation: A transport (other than a barge or vessel) that meets these qualifications will be considered "bulk packaging". A receptacle for liquids with a maximum capacity greater than 119 gallons (450 L). A receptacle for solids that has a net mass greater than 882 pounds (400 kg) and a maximum capacity greater than 119 gallons (450 L). A water capacity greater than 1,000 pounds (454 kg) is a receptacle for gas.

explanation: A transport (other than a barge or vessel) that meets these qualifications will be considered "bulk packaging". A receptacle for liquids with a maximum capacity greater than 119 gallons (450 L). A receptacle for solids that has a net mass greater than 882 pounds (400 kg) and a maximum capacity greater than 119 gallons (450 L). A water capacity greater than 1,000 pounds (454 kg) is a receptacle for gas.

question: What is the purpose of the shipper signing the shipping paperwork?

answers.text: To prove when the paperwork was created for tracking reported quantities of hazardous materials
This self-certification states that the shipper has followed all regulations during preparation of the shipment
answers.explanation: The signature is a type of self-certification and officially declares that the shipper has followed all federal requirements when preparing the shipment.

answers.text: This self-certification states that the amounts of materials reported on the shipping paperwork match the package contents

explanation: The signature is a type of self-certification and officially declares that the shipper has followed all federal requirements when preparing the shipment.

question: Under which situation would you be allowed to move an improperly placarded vehicle?

answers.text: Never move an improperly placarded vehicle
In an emergency, to move it out of harm’s way
answers.explanation: In an emergency situation it may be necessary to move vehicles out of harm’s way, such as an empty vehicle parked in a loading dock when a fire breaks out.

answers.text: Whenever you have a hazardous materials endorsement

explanation: In an emergency situation it may be necessary to move vehicles out of harm’s way, such as an empty vehicle parked in a loading dock when a fire breaks out.

question: An X in the hazardous materials column of a shipping paper entry means:

answers.text: This is an exception to the hazardous materials regulations.
The material is not a hazardous material.
The entry is for a hazardous material.
answers.explanation: An X or RQ can identify a substance regulated by hazardous materials laws.

explanation: An X or RQ can identify a substance regulated by hazardous materials laws.

question: What is the appropriate placarding when a vehicle has 1,000 pounds of flammable gases and 1,000 pounds of non-flammable gases?

answers.text: Use both the Flammable Gas and Non-Flammable Gas placards
answers.explanation: Since both are from table 2 and the total weight of hazardous materials is over 1,000 pounds, the vehicle will require both the Flammable Gas and Non-Flammable Gas placards.

answers.text: The Dangerous placard will accommodate both types
Use the Combustible placard with any other required placards.

explanation: Since both are from table 2 and the total weight of hazardous materials is over 1,000 pounds, the vehicle will require both the Flammable Gas and Non-Flammable Gas placards.

question: Defining differences between a portable tank and a cargo tank include:

answers.text: Cargo tanks are filled off the vehicle and are permanently attached.
Portable tanks are filled on or off the vehicle and are only temporarily attached.
answers.explanation: Cargo tanks are permanently attached and filled on the vehicle. Portable tanks can be filled on or off the vehicle and are temporarily attached. Portable tanks must also display the owner’s name.

answers.text: Cargo tanks must display the owner's name.

explanation: Cargo tanks are permanently attached and filled on the vehicle. Portable tanks can be filled on or off the vehicle and are temporarily attached. Portable tanks must also display the owner’s name.

question: What protocol should be followed when you are given a package that is leaking?

answers.text: Place the package into a new package before transporting
Refuse the package from the shipper
answers.explanation: Refuse/return the package to the shipper and let them handle the issue.

explanation: Refuse/return the package to the shipper and let them handle the issue.

question: What is the minimum amount of materials that can be transported from hazardous materials table 1 before you need a placard?

answers.text: Any weight from a table 1 material requires a placard
answers.explanation: Materials from table 1 require placards for any amount of the hazardous material.

explanation: Materials from table 1 require placards for any amount of the hazardous material.

question: Animal and human foodstuffs should not be loaded in the same vehicle with:

answers.explanation: Never load human or animal foodstuffs (food that is swallowed) with poison cargos unless the poison packaging is over-packed in an approved container. Hygiene products like toothpaste or mouthwash are not classified as foodstuffs.

explanation: Never load human or animal foodstuffs (food that is swallowed) with poison cargos unless the poison packaging is over-packed in an approved container. Hygiene products like toothpaste or mouthwash are not classified as foodstuffs.

question: Carriers must give each driver who transports Class 1.1 or 1.2 explosives:

answers.text: A copy of FMCSR (Federal Motor Carrier Safety Regulation) part 397.
answers.explanation: FMCSR Part 397 describes the rules and regulations for transporting and parking hazardous materials. A carrier assigning a driver a Division 1.1, 1.2, or 1.3 explosives cargo must also provide the driver with FMCSR Part 397 and the driver must sign a receipt indicating that they have received it.

answers.text: A list of rest stops the driver may use.

explanation: FMCSR Part 397 describes the rules and regulations for transporting and parking hazardous materials. A carrier assigning a driver a Division 1.1, 1.2, or 1.3 explosives cargo must also provide the driver with FMCSR Part 397 and the driver must sign a receipt indicating that they have received it.

question: What is the appropriate action to take when you realized that an enclosed trailer has a fire inside?

answers.text: Take the fire extinguisher into the trailer to put out the fire
Stop and disconnect the trailer and then pull the tractor forward to avoid the fire.
Keep the trailer doors closed and call emergency services
answers.explanation: The recommended action is to keep the trailer doors closed and call emergency services. Opening the doors will give the fire more air to continue to burn and burn hotter.

explanation: The recommended action is to keep the trailer doors closed and call emergency services. Opening the doors will give the fire more air to continue to burn and burn hotter.

question: A properly prepared Uniform Hazardous Waste Manifest:

answers.text: Must be signed and carried by anyone transporting a hazardous waste.
answers.explanation: Every driver transporting hazardous waste must sign the Uniform Hazardous Waste Manifest and carry it for the duration of the transport.

answers.text: Is the same as any other shipping paper/
Is required only If there is a loss of cargo during transport.

explanation: Every driver transporting hazardous waste must sign the Uniform Hazardous Waste Manifest and carry it for the duration of the transport.

question: The US Department of Transportation has designated over 3,000 different types of materials to be hazardous. Specifics about each material is found in the Hazardous Materials Table, which has a total of ______ columns.

answers.explanation: There are eight columns in the Hazardous Materials Table that are used to communicate the mode of transportation, material name, hazard class, ID number, and more. Refer to the driver handbook for details.

explanation: There are eight columns in the Hazardous Materials Table that are used to communicate the mode of transportation, material name, hazard class, ID number, and more. Refer to the driver handbook for details.

question: You are going to be hauling hazardous materials over a route you do not know well. When should you check the route and the permits needed for the trip?

answers.text: While you are still on a part of the route that you know.

answers.explanation: Local and state regulations for the transportation of hazardous materials will vary. If a route is planned through an area where you are unfamiliar with the regulations it is the driver's responsibility to check the laws for restrictions prior to beginning the trip.

answers.text: Within 24 hours of the end of the trip.

explanation: Local and state regulations for the transportation of hazardous materials will vary. If a route is planned through an area where you are unfamiliar with the regulations it is the driver's responsibility to check the laws for restrictions prior to beginning the trip.

question: Which statement is true regarding the California Commercial Driver Handbook?

answers.text: The handbook includes detailed containment procedures for when a HazMat accident occurs.
The handbook will tell a driver everything they will ever need to know about hazardous materials transportation.
The handbook provides everything you currently need to know to obtain a hazardous materials endorsement.
answers.explanation: Laws and technology change over time. The handbook provides everything you currently need to know to obtain a hazardous materials endorsement. The handbook does get updated to reflect changes when they occur, and it is up to drivers to stay abreast of those changes.

explanation: Laws and technology change over time. The handbook provides everything you currently need to know to obtain a hazardous materials endorsement. The handbook does get updated to reflect changes when they occur, and it is up to drivers to stay abreast of those changes.

question: Which party is responsible for correctly completing shipping papers, packaging the materials, and applying the correct labels?

answers.explanation: Shippers are responsible for correctly completing the shipping papers, packaging the materials, and applying the correct labels. The driver then validates each piece of information to ensure that the cargo is ready for transportation.

explanation: Shippers are responsible for correctly completing the shipping papers, packaging the materials, and applying the correct labels. The driver then validates each piece of information to ensure that the cargo is ready for transportation.

question: What does the letter "G" indicate in the first column of the Hazardous Materials Table?

answers.text: Means the HazMat described in Column 2 is a generic shipping name.
answers.explanation: The letter "G" in the first column of the Hazardous Materials Table indicates that the HazMat described in column 2 is a generic shipping name.

answers.text: Identifies a proper shipping name that is used to describe materials in international transportation.
Shows the proper shipping name, hazard class, and packing group to use, even if the material does not meet the hazard class definition.

explanation: The letter "G" in the first column of the Hazardous Materials Table indicates that the HazMat described in column 2 is a generic shipping name.

question: When an identification number is preceded by NA, this shipment is in which geographical area?

answers.explanation: International shipments may have the letters UN or ID. NA is specific to the North American countries of the United States and Canada.

explanation: International shipments may have the letters UN or ID. NA is specific to the North American countries of the United States and Canada.

question: The intent of the Hazardous Materials regulation has three parts: (1) to ensure safe drivers and equipment (2) to contain the materials and (3)?

answers.explanation: Regulations of Hazardous Materials seek to contain the materials, communicate the risk, and ensure the safety of the drivers and equipment.

explanation: Regulations of Hazardous Materials seek to contain the materials, communicate the risk, and ensure the safety of the drivers and equipment.

question: You may not park a vehicle carrying hazardous materials within ____ feet of an open fire.

answers.explanation: When carrying hazardous materials the following should be avoided unless absolutely necessary. Maintain a distance of 300 feet from any open flame, places where people gather, along with buildings, tunnels, or bridges.

explanation: When carrying hazardous materials the following should be avoided unless absolutely necessary. Maintain a distance of 300 feet from any open flame, places where people gather, along with buildings, tunnels, or bridges.

question: You have a vehicle without racks to hold cylinders of compressed gas. You may load such cylinders only if they are:

answers.text: Less than a half of the load.
Loaded upright or braced laying down flat.
answers.explanation: If your vehicle does not have racks to hold cylinders, the cargo space floor must be flat. The cylinders must be: (1) Held upright. (2) Cylinders may be loaded in a horizontal position (lying down) if it is designed so the relief valve is in the vapor space.

explanation: If your vehicle does not have racks to hold cylinders, the cargo space floor must be flat. The cylinders must be: (1) Held upright. (2) Cylinders may be loaded in a horizontal position (lying down) if it is designed so the relief valve is in the vapor space.

question: With most hazardous materials, you may park within 5 feet of the road briefly, if your work requires it. The exceptions are:

answers.explanation: Do not park close to the roadway when transporting from Division 1.1, 1.2, or 1.3 explosives. Ensure that you are more than 5 feet off the roadway.

explanation: Do not park close to the roadway when transporting from Division 1.1, 1.2, or 1.3 explosives. Ensure that you are more than 5 feet off the roadway.

question: Your vehicle contains explosives, oxidizer, or flammables. No one may smoke within ____ feet of the vehicle.

answers.explanation: When transporting flammable liquids (Class 3) or gases (Division 2.1), do not allow anyone to smoke within 25 feet of the placarded cargo tank.

explanation: When transporting flammable liquids (Class 3) or gases (Division 2.1), do not allow anyone to smoke within 25 feet of the placarded cargo tank.

question: A vehicle placarded for hazardous materials must have placards on ____ sides.

answers.explanation: HazMat placards placed on all four sides of the vehicle to indicate the classification of the substance. This will be on both sides of the vehicle plus the front and rear.

explanation: HazMat placards placed on all four sides of the vehicle to indicate the classification of the substance. This will be on both sides of the vehicle plus the front and rear.

question: Which of the following is true regarding federal regulations and hazardous materials?

answers.text: They make it easy to identify hazardous materials during transport.
answers.explanation: Federal regulations make it easy for shippers to identify hazardous materials on paperwork and for transportation companies to easily identify hazardous materials in transit.

answers.text: Regulations keep shipments private between the shipper and receiver.
They make it easy to track the locations of shipments.

explanation: Federal regulations make it easy for shippers to identify hazardous materials on paperwork and for transportation companies to easily identify hazardous materials in transit.

question: Which product may not be transported with charged storage batteries?

answers.explanation: Charged storage batteries cannot be transported with mass explosives as they can potentially trigger an explosion if combined.

explanation: Charged storage batteries cannot be transported with mass explosives as they can potentially trigger an explosion if combined.

question: You should stop ____ feet before the nearest rail.

answers.explanation: When approaching a railroad crossing while transporting hazardous materials, be sure to stop at least 15 to 50 feet before the nearest rail.

explanation: When approaching a railroad crossing while transporting hazardous materials, be sure to stop at least 15 to 50 feet before the nearest rail.

question: Which statement is true of cargo tanks?

answers.text: They are only temporarily attached to the vehicle
They are only filled while attached to the vehicle
answers.explanation: Unlike portable tanks, cargo tanks are filled while attached to the vehicle and they are always permanently attached.

answers.text: They may be filled attached or unattached to the vehicle

explanation: Unlike portable tanks, cargo tanks are filled while attached to the vehicle and they are always permanently attached.

question: Only one of these shipping papers description for a hazardous material is in the right order. Which one is it?

answers.explanation: When a shipper completes the shipping label the correct order of information on the label is the ID number first, then the proper shipping name, followed by the hazard classification or division, then packaging group.

explanation: When a shipper completes the shipping label the correct order of information on the label is the ID number first, then the proper shipping name, followed by the hazard classification or division, then packaging group.

question: The basic description of a hazardous material includes the hazard class, the identification number and the proper shipping name. Which one must appear first in the shipping paper?

answers.explanation: When a shipper completes the shipping label the correct order of information on the label is the ID number first, then the proper shipping name, followed by the hazard classification or division, and packaging group.

explanation: When a shipper completes the shipping label the correct order of information on the label is the ID number first, then the proper shipping name, followed by the hazard classification or division, and packaging group.

question: Who is responsible for finding out what permits or special routes you will need to haul hazardous materials?

answers.explanation: Local and state regulations for the transportation of hazardous materials will vary. If a route is planned through an area where you are unfamiliar with the regulations it is the driver's responsibility to check the laws for restrictions prior to beginning the trip.

explanation: Local and state regulations for the transportation of hazardous materials will vary. If a route is planned through an area where you are unfamiliar with the regulations it is the driver's responsibility to check the laws for restrictions prior to beginning the trip.

question: When fueling a placarded vehicle someone must always be:

answers.text: At the nozzle, controlling the fuel flow.
answers.explanation: To avoid the potential for accidents while fueling a placarded vehicle, never smoke around the vehicle and someone must always be at the nozzle controlling the fuel flow.

answers.text: Within 10 feet of the pump with a fire extinguisher.
At the emergency power shut-off for the pump.

explanation: To avoid the potential for accidents while fueling a placarded vehicle, never smoke around the vehicle and someone must always be at the nozzle controlling the fuel flow.

question: There are two lists that drivers, shippers and carriers use to find out if a material is a regulated product. One of these is the:

answers.text: EPA (U.S. Environmental Protection Agency) Dangerous Materials Table.
List of Hazardous Materials and Reportable Quantities
answers.explanation: Drivers, carriers, and shippers refer to the List of Marine Pollutants, List of Hazardous Substances and Reportable Quantities, and the Hazardous Materials Table to identify hazardous materials. Ensure that you refer to the lists for identification before taking possession of any cargo.

explanation: Drivers, carriers, and shippers refer to the List of Marine Pollutants, List of Hazardous Substances and Reportable Quantities, and the Hazardous Materials Table to identify hazardous materials. Ensure that you refer to the lists for identification before taking possession of any cargo.

question: How often do HazMat employees need to receive remedial, continuous, or retraining?

answers.explanation: Federal regulations require that HazMat employees be retrained every three years. State or local regulations can require additional training. If an employer changes their security plan, then all employees must be retrained within 90 days.

explanation: Federal regulations require that HazMat employees be retrained every three years. State or local regulations can require additional training. If an employer changes their security plan, then all employees must be retrained within 90 days.

question: The hazardous materials endorsement testing requires a driver to demonstrate knowledge of:

answers.text: the various hazard classes of materials
answers.explanation: The "H" endorsement requires a candidate to pass a test to demonstrate knowledge of hazardous materials classes, how to placard a vehicle, how to properly load hazardous materials packages, and how to safely transport packages.

explanation: The "H" endorsement requires a candidate to pass a test to demonstrate knowledge of hazardous materials classes, how to placard a vehicle, how to properly load hazardous materials packages, and how to safely transport packages.

question: You are transporting Division 1.2 or 1.3 materials. How far away from bridges, tunnels, and buildings must you park whenever you stop for a break?

answers.explanation: You must park at least 300 feet away from any buildings, bridges, or tunnels. open fires or gathering of people whenever you stop while transporting Division 1.2, or 1.3 hazardous materials.

explanation: You must park at least 300 feet away from any buildings, bridges, or tunnels. open fires or gathering of people whenever you stop while transporting Division 1.2, or 1.3 hazardous materials.

question: What is the closest you may park to a roadway when transporting Division 1.2 or 1.3 dangerous materials?

answers.explanation: The closest that you may park to a roadway when transporting Division 1.2 or 1.3 explosives is 5 feet. If you cannot get at least 5 feet from the road you must call emergency services for assistance.

explanation: The closest that you may park to a roadway when transporting Division 1.2 or 1.3 explosives is 5 feet. If you cannot get at least 5 feet from the road you must call emergency services for assistance.

question: Which statement is true of fueling a placarded CMV?

answers.text: The 10B:C fire extinguisher needs to be placed next to the fuel tanks during filling
The engine must be off and someone holding the nozzle to control the fuel flow
answers.explanation: Any placarded vehicle (hazardous materials cargo) requires that the engine be off, and someone must be holding the nozzle and controlling the fuel flow into the tank.

answers.text: A static strap must be used between the pump and fuel tank to eliminate static energy

explanation: Any placarded vehicle (hazardous materials cargo) requires that the engine be off, and someone must be holding the nozzle and controlling the fuel flow into the tank.

question: Hazardous materials identification numbers placed on cargo tanks, portable tanks, and bulk packaging must be of which size?

answers.explanation: The hazardous materials identification numbers displayed on cargo tanks, portable tanks, and bulk packaging must be black and 100 mm.

explanation: The hazardous materials identification numbers displayed on cargo tanks, portable tanks, and bulk packaging must be black and 100 mm.

question: You are driving a vehicle loaded with explosives, when is it safe to leave the vehicle unattended?

answers.text: When parked in a designated safe haven.
answers.explanation: The local authorities typically identify "safe havens" where vehicles transporting explosives may be parked unattended. A safe haven is the only location where a driver may park an explosives cargo unattended.

answers.text: When parked in a truck stop.

explanation: The local authorities typically identify "safe havens" where vehicles transporting explosives may be parked unattended. A safe haven is the only location where a driver may park an explosives cargo unattended.

question: Who is responsible for packaging, labeling and preparing the hazardous material shipping papers for a common carrier?

answers.explanation: Shippers are responsible for the packaging, labeling, and preparing the hazardous material shipping papers. Packing and shipping labels must match, so ensure they do when you take possession of the cargo. Other information may include the name and address of the shipper or consignee, the HazMat shipping name and ID, and the appropriate labels.

explanation: Shippers are responsible for the packaging, labeling, and preparing the hazardous material shipping papers. Packing and shipping labels must match, so ensure they do when you take possession of the cargo. Other information may include the name and address of the shipper or consignee, the HazMat shipping name and ID, and the appropriate labels.

question: Where should hazardous materials shipping papers be kept whenever you are parked and leaving the vehicle unattended?

answers.explanation: Whenever the vehicle is unattended, you must leave the hazardous materials shipping paperwork on the driver's seat so that anyone can access them in an emergency.

answers.text: In the center console or glove compartment

explanation: Whenever the vehicle is unattended, you must leave the hazardous materials shipping paperwork on the driver's seat so that anyone can access them in an emergency.

question: Regarding the Hazardous Materials Table, which designator will appear in column 1 when the material is subject to the HMR only when offered or intended for transport by air unless it is a hazardous substance or hazardous waste?

answers.explanation: The letter designation of "A" is used when the material is subject to the HMR only when offered or intended for transport by air unless it is a hazardous substance or hazardous waste.

explanation: The letter designation of "A" is used when the material is subject to the HMR only when offered or intended for transport by air unless it is a hazardous substance or hazardous waste.

question: A copy of which document must carriers provide to a driver that will be transporting Division 1.1, 1.2, or 1.3 materials?

answers.explanation: FMCSR Part 397 refers to the Transportation of Hazardous Materials: Driving and Park Rules. A copy of this document must be provided by any carrier who assigns a driver a trip transporting Division 1.1, 1.2, or 1.3 hazardous materials.

explanation: FMCSR Part 397 refers to the Transportation of Hazardous Materials: Driving and Park Rules. A copy of this document must be provided by any carrier who assigns a driver a trip transporting Division 1.1, 1.2, or 1.3 hazardous materials.

question: The power unit of a placarded vehicle must have a fire extinguisher with a UL rating of ____ B:C or more.

answers.explanation: A fire extinguisher with a minimum UL rating of 10 B:C or greater is required when transporting placarded cargo.

explanation: A fire extinguisher with a minimum UL rating of 10 B:C or greater is required when transporting placarded cargo.

question: A qualified person must be within _____ feet of a cargo tank while loading or unloading.

answers.explanation: A qualified person must be authorized to move the cargo and have the required knowledge of the hazards to properly react. This qualified person must remain within 25 feet of the cargo tank while loading or unloading.

explanation: A qualified person must be authorized to move the cargo and have the required knowledge of the hazards to properly react. This qualified person must remain within 25 feet of the cargo tank while loading or unloading.

question: When shippers package hazardous materials, they must certify that this was done according to regulations. The only exception is when:

answers.text: The driver is given a sealed cargo compartment.
The shipment is a hazardous waste.
The shipper is a private carrier carrying their own product.
answers.explanation: Shipping papers must be certified that they have followed all labeling instructions for hazardous materials except when the shipper is a private carrier and transporting their own products.

explanation: Shipping papers must be certified that they have followed all labeling instructions for hazardous materials except when the shipper is a private carrier and transporting their own products.

question: Is it necessary to stop at a railroad crossing when hauling class 7 hazardous materials while placarded?

answers.explanation: All placarded CMV's must stop 15 to 50 feet before the nearest railroad track and verify that no train is coming. Be sure that you can traverse the entire crossing before moving forward.

answers.text: No, only when the traffic control device indicates a train

explanation: All placarded CMV's must stop 15 to 50 feet before the nearest railroad track and verify that no train is coming. Be sure that you can traverse the entire crossing before moving forward.

question: Which of the following must drivers have in their possession while transporting Class 1.1 or 1.2 explosives?

answers.explanation: A written route plan must be created when transporting explosives (Division 1.1, 1.2, or 1.3). The driver must have the route plan with them and follow it.

explanation: A written route plan must be created when transporting explosives (Division 1.1, 1.2, or 1.3). The driver must have the route plan with them and follow it.

question: You are hauling hazardous materials and you find that one of your tires is leaking, you must:

answers.text: Stop at the nearest safe place and fix it.
answers.explanation: Tire failures may cause accidents to occur. If a driver notices that a tire is damaged or leaking air while they are transporting hazardous materials, they must stop and seek repair for the tire before proceeding. This will mean following appropriate parking or delay requirements for the hazardous cargo being transported.

answers.text: Report it to your carrier immediately,
Continue at reduced speed, and check that tire every 25 miles.

explanation: Tire failures may cause accidents to occur. If a driver notices that a tire is damaged or leaking air while they are transporting hazardous materials, they must stop and seek repair for the tire before proceeding. This will mean following appropriate parking or delay requirements for the hazardous cargo being transported.

question: It is necessary for the engine to run to operate a pump for delivering compressed gas. When in the process should you turn off the engine?

answers.explanation: The engine in this situation causes a hazard. The engine should be turned off prior to disconnecting the hose to ensure that it is not generating any more pressure.

answers.text: Neither answer A, nor B, is correct

explanation: The engine in this situation causes a hazard. The engine should be turned off prior to disconnecting the hose to ensure that it is not generating any more pressure.

question: A dated certificate of radioactive materials training must be:

answers.explanation: By law, a driver who transports controlled radioactive material must be trained in the security, transportation, handling, and response to the substance being carried every three years. The driver must carry the training certification when transporting these materials.

explanation: By law, a driver who transports controlled radioactive material must be trained in the security, transportation, handling, and response to the substance being carried every three years. The driver must carry the training certification when transporting these materials.

question: Nitric acid should not be loaded:

answers.explanation: Nitric acid is a class 8 material, meaning that it is corrosive and shall not be loaded above any other product.

explanation: Nitric acid is a class 8 material, meaning that it is corrosive and shall not be loaded above any other product.

question: Which rules apply to the packaging, loading, transportation, and unloading of hazardous materials?

answers.explanation: Containment rules describe directions for packaging, loading, transporting, and unloading hazardous materials.

explanation: Containment rules describe directions for packaging, loading, transporting, and unloading hazardous materials.

question: Which party is responsible for signing the hazardous waste manifest?

answers.text: The shipper, the receiver, and all carriers who transported the material
answers.explanation: The shipper, the receiver, and all carriers who transported the material must sign the hazardous waste manifest. You should always keep your copy of the signed manifest for proof.

answers.text: The shipper when preparing the cargo and the receiver once the cargo is delivered
The shipper and all carriers who transported the material

explanation: The shipper, the receiver, and all carriers who transported the material must sign the hazardous waste manifest. You should always keep your copy of the signed manifest for proof.

question: If you need to placard a vehicle for hazardous materials, how many placards will you need?

answers.explanation: HazMat placards are placed on all four sides of the vehicle to indicate the classification of the substance. This will be on both sides of the vehicle plus the front and rear.

explanation: HazMat placards are placed on all four sides of the vehicle to indicate the classification of the substance. This will be on both sides of the vehicle plus the front and rear.

question: Which column of the Hazardous Materials Table lists the material ID number?

answers.explanation: Column four of the Hazardous Materials Table contains the unique material identification number.

explanation: Column four of the Hazardous Materials Table contains the unique material identification number.

question: Class 1.1 explosives must not be transported in a combination vehicle if it includes:

answers.explanation: Regulations dictate that Division 1.1 or 1.2 explosives may not be transported in combination with a placarded cargo tank.

explanation: Regulations dictate that Division 1.1 or 1.2 explosives may not be transported in combination with a placarded cargo tank.

question: Which of the following is true of the Emergency Response Guidebook used by first responders to HazMat situations?

answers.text: The guidebook is organized by hazardous material classification number.
answers.explanation: The ERG was written by the US Department of Transportation and is used across the entire nation. The guidebook is a quick reference for firefighters and EMS personnel to inform them of how to react to each hazard classification.

answers.text: The guidebook is only used for certain states within the US.
The guidebook was written by the National Highway Traffic Safety Association.

explanation: The ERG was written by the US Department of Transportation and is used across the entire nation. The guidebook is a quick reference for firefighters and EMS personnel to inform them of how to react to each hazard classification.

question: A vehicle contains 500 pounds each or explosives class 1.1 and 1.2. You must use:

answers.explanation: This cargo will require explosive placards. Refer to the placard table for a list of materials and the required placards.

explanation: This cargo will require explosive placards. Refer to the placard table for a list of materials and the required placards.

question: When handling packages of explosives, you must:

answers.text: Double wrap wet boxes in plastic to prevent staining.
Roll the packages carefully, with no sharp or jarring actions.
Never use hooks or other metal tools.
answers.explanation: Obviously, explosives are sensitive materials and many can combust from things other than just open flames. Do not throw, roll, or toss these packages and do not use hooks or metal tools that may puncture the packaging.

explanation: Obviously, explosives are sensitive materials and many can combust from things other than just open flames. Do not throw, roll, or toss these packages and do not use hooks or metal tools that may puncture the packaging.

question: Within which area must a placarded CMV stop when approaching a railroad crossing?

answers.explanation: All placarded CMV's must stop 15 to 50 feet of the nearest rail when approaching a railroad crossing.

explanation: All placarded CMV's must stop 15 to 50 feet of the nearest rail when approaching a railroad crossing.

question: You find an overheated tire during inspection. If you are hauling hazardous materials, you must:

answers.text: Cool the tire, then check it every 2 hours.
Remove the tire and place it at a distance from the vehicle.
answers.explanation: Tire failures may cause accidents to occur. If a driver notices that a tire is damaged or leaking air while they are transporting hazardous materials, they must stop and seek repair for the tire before proceeding. This will mean following appropriate parking or delay requirements for the hazardous cargo being transported.

answers.text: Wait at least 2 hours before continuing on your trip.

explanation: Tire failures may cause accidents to occur. If a driver notices that a tire is damaged or leaking air while they are transporting hazardous materials, they must stop and seek repair for the tire before proceeding. This will mean following appropriate parking or delay requirements for the hazardous cargo being transported.

question: Regarding shipping paperwork and hazmat tables, a technical name:

answers.text: Identifies the exact chemical that makes a hazardous material dangerous.
Identifies the chemical elements on the periodic table.
answers.explanation:
A generic shipping name must be accompanied by a technical name on the shipping paper. A technical name is a specific chemical that makes the product
hazardous.

explanation:
A generic shipping name must be accompanied by a technical name on the shipping paper. A technical name is a specific chemical that makes the product
hazardous.

question: What is the appropriate response to a hazardous materials emergency?

answers.text: Secure the site and keep people away from harm
answers.explanation: In the event of a hazardous materials emergency, secure the accident site and keep people away from harm. Ensure that nobody is smoking and that no open flames are near the materials. Follow directions given by your employer and the authorities.

answers.text: Disconnect the trailer or cargo tanker and drive the tractor out of the exposure area.
Place flares near the hazard to warn approaching drivers.

explanation: In the event of a hazardous materials emergency, secure the accident site and keep people away from harm. Ensure that nobody is smoking and that no open flames are near the materials. Follow directions given by your employer and the authorities.

question: Regarding placard table 2, at which weight limit are you required to use placards?

answers.explanation: Starting at 1,001 total pounds, the transportation of any table 2 materials will require placards.

explanation: Starting at 1,001 total pounds, the transportation of any table 2 materials will require placards.

question: When transporting Division 1.1 or 1.2 materials, which material makes a suitable floor liner?

answers.explanation: Division 1.1 and 1.2 explosives require a nonmetallic material or non-ferrous metal to be used as a floor lining or when moving materials.

explanation: Division 1.1 and 1.2 explosives require a nonmetallic material or non-ferrous metal to be used as a floor lining or when moving materials.

question: What is the minimum inspection interval for checking the tires of a placarded tractor-trailer with dual wheels?

answers.text: Prior to the start of a trip and then at each stop
answers.explanation: The tires of a placarded tractor-trailer with dual wheels must be inspected prior to starting a trip and then at each stop

explanation: The tires of a placarded tractor-trailer with dual wheels must be inspected prior to starting a trip and then at each stop

question: Who is responsible for checking that the shipper correctly named, labeled, and marked a hazardous materials shipment?

answers.explanation: The driver is a representative of the carrier. Prior to taking possession of any cargo, it is the carrier's responsibility to verify the shipper properly named, labeled, and marked any hazardous materials shipments.

explanation: The driver is a representative of the carrier. Prior to taking possession of any cargo, it is the carrier's responsibility to verify the shipper properly named, labeled, and marked any hazardous materials shipments.

question: What is the minimum UL rating that a fire extinguisher may have for a placarded transportation?

answers.explanation: The minimum UL fire extinguisher rating allowed for placarded vehicles is a 10B:C (10 square foot and deployable against most flammable liquids and electrically nonconductive materials).

explanation: The minimum UL fire extinguisher rating allowed for placarded vehicles is a 10B:C (10 square foot and deployable against most flammable liquids and electrically nonconductive materials).

question: When may you allow another person to attend to your vehicle?

answers.text: Whenever you stop to take a break
When on the property of the receiver, carrier, or shipper
answers.explanation: The driver is responsible for getting the cargo to the destination. Only allow another person to attend your vehicle when you are on the property of the shipper, carrier, or receiver

answers.text: When it is another CDL holder

explanation: The driver is responsible for getting the cargo to the destination. Only allow another person to attend your vehicle when you are on the property of the shipper, carrier, or receiver

question: A driver who transports controlled radioactive material must have had special training by the carrier within the last ____ years.

answers.explanation: By law a driver who transports controlled radioactive material must be trained in the security, transportation, handling, and response to the substance being carried every three years.

explanation: By law a driver who transports controlled radioactive material must be trained in the security, transportation, handling, and response to the substance being carried every three years.
